questions.q: Shikhandi was the rebirth of which wronged princess?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi was Amba reborn, carrying her vow to destroy Bhishma.

questions.q: Shikhandi was born as a child of which king?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi was born in the house of Drupada, king of Panchala.

questions.q: Whose destruction was the single purpose of Shikhandi's birth?
questions.explanation: Shiva had promised Amba that in her next life she would cause Bhishma's death.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma refuse to raise his weapons against Shikhandi?
options: Shikhandi had once saved his life
Shikhandi had been born a woman
questions.explanation: Bhishma knew Shikhandi had been born a woman and refused to fight such a foe.

questions.q: On which side did Shikhandi fight in the Kurukshetra war?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi fought for the Pandavas, the husbands of his sister Draupadi.

questions.q: Which famous pair were Shikhandi's siblings, born of the same father?
questions.explanation: Dhrishtadyumna and Draupadi, Drupada's fire-born children, were Shikhandi's siblings.

questions.q: Of which kingdom was Shikhandi a prince?
questions.explanation: Shikhandi was a prince of Panchala, Drupada's kingdom.

questions.q: On the tenth day of the war, where did Shikhandi stand?
options: In front of Arjuna, facing Bhishma
On the wing with the cavalry
questions.explanation: Arjuna fought from behind Shikhandi, whom Bhishma would not strike.

questions.q: In the previous life, from what ceremony did Bhishma abduct the princess who became Shikhandi?
questions.explanation: Bhishma carried off Amba and her sisters from their swayamvara at Kashi for his brother's wedding.

questions.q: Although born a girl, how was Shikhandi raised by Drupada?
questions.explanation: Following a divine promise, Drupada raised the girl as a son trained in arms.

questions.q: Which yaksha gave Shikhandi manhood?
questions.explanation: The yaksha Sthunakarna lent Shikhandi his manhood in the forest.

questions.q: What exactly did the yaksha exchange with Shikhandini in the forest?
options: His male form for her female form
questions.explanation: Sthunakarna took Shikhandi's female form and gave his male form in exchange.

questions.q: Who cursed the yaksha so that the exchange would not end at the agreed time?
questions.explanation: Kubera, lord of the yakshas, cursed Sthunakarna to keep the female form until Shikhandi died.

questions.q: Which king threatened war on Drupada after his daughter discovered the truth about her new spouse?
questions.explanation: Hiranyavarman of Dasharna, the bride's father, marched on Panchala in fury.

questions.q: In the former life, which king did Amba love and hope to wed?
questions.explanation: Amba loved Shalva of Saubha, who rejected her after the abduction.

questions.q: Which god finally promised Amba that she would cause Bhishma's death in another life?
questions.explanation: After her long austerities, Shiva granted Amba the boon of revenge in her next birth.

questions.q: How did Amba end her first life to hasten her revenge?
options: She drowned herself in the Ganga
She starved on a mountain peak
She fell on Bhishma's own sword
She walked into a blazing fire
questions.explanation: Amba built a pyre and entered the fire to hasten her rebirth.

questions.q: What did Bhishma do whenever Shikhandi confronted him on the battlefield?
options: He shot only at Shikhandi's banner
He lowered his weapons and would not strike
He called for a temporary truce
questions.explanation: Remembering the woman's birth, Bhishma dropped his bow whenever Shikhandi faced him.

questions.q: Who fought from behind Shikhandi on the day Bhishma fell?
questions.explanation: Arjuna shot from behind Shikhandi on the day the grandsire fell.

questions.q: How did Shikhandi die?
options: Beheaded by Karna on the seventeenth day
Slain by Ashwatthama in the night raid
Burned in the camp fire after the war
questions.explanation: Ashwatthama slaughtered Shikhandi along with the camp's sleeping warriors in the night raid.

questions.q: In the previous life, Amba was the eldest princess of which city, with sisters Ambika and Ambalika?
questions.explanation: Amba was the eldest of the three Kashi princesses Bhishma carried off.

questions.q: Which warrior took up Amba's cause and fought Bhishma on her behalf in her former life?
questions.explanation: Parashurama, Bhishma's own guru, championed Amba and fought him for many days.

questions.q: How did the long duel between Parashurama and Bhishma over Amba finally end?
options: Parashurama slew Bhishma's charioteer and won
Bhishma surrendered out of respect for his guru
It was halted as a stalemate with no victor
Amba herself stopped it by withdrawing her claim
questions.explanation: After twenty-three days the gods intervened, and the duel ended with neither able to win.

questions.q: What was Shikhandi's birth name as a girl in Drupada's house?
questions.explanation: The child was named Shikhandini and later took the name Shikhandi.

questions.q: What did a divine voice tell Drupada at the child's birth?
options: To hide the child in the forest
To offer the child to Bhishma's service
To send the child to Kashi at once
To raise the daughter as a son
questions.explanation: A voice from the sky told Drupada the child was a daughter who must be raised as a son.

questions.q: Pierced on the tenth day, Bhishma declared that only one warrior's arrows were striking him to the quick. Whose?
questions.explanation: Bhishma said the arrows striking like thunderbolts were Arjuna's, never Shikhandi's.

questions.q: When the Pandavas asked Bhishma himself how he might be brought down, what counsel did he give?
options: Place Shikhandi at the front and shoot from behind him
Attack him only after sunset when the code lapsed
Send Krishna to face him with the discus
Challenge him to single combat with maces
questions.explanation: Bhishma himself told the Pandavas to place Shikhandi before Arjuna, for he would not fight him.

questions.q: How was Shikhandi's manhood finally proven to the furious king of Dasharna?
options: Bhishma himself vouched for it in writing
Drupada swore an oath before the sacred fire
Young women sent by the king verified it
The yaksha appeared at court as witness
questions.explanation: Hiranyavarman sent young women, who confirmed that Shikhandi was a man, averting the war.

questions.q: According to the Udyoga Parva, what became of part of Amba after her fierce austerities?
options: She rose to Indra's heaven as an apsara
She turned into a flowering shami tree
She merged into the blade of a sword
She became a river in the Vatsa country
questions.explanation: The Udyoga Parva says part of Amba became a river in the Vatsa country after her austerities.

questions.q: Under Kubera's curse, how long would the yaksha keep Shikhandi's female form?
options: Until the war at Kurukshetra ended
Until the day of Shikhandi's death
For exactly thirteen years of exile
Until Bhishma fell on the field
questions.explanation: Kubera decreed the exchange would last until Shikhandi's death, so Shikhandi remained a man for life.
